notes: Lesson 3 Part 3 (act 53, 'Taxes create a wedge and split burden', 291.7s, 14 cues rescaled to real MP3). Tax-incidence focus using econ.py tax_wedge / burden_split / steep_demand on the shared market (per-unit tax 2 → Q 3.75, buyer 6, seller 4, wedge 2). Two iterations: round-1 authored+rendered; round-2 fixed several idiom/layout issues — tax_wedge brace flipped to the LEFT + larger label so it no longer crowds the equilibrium crossing (Q_TAX 3.75 sits near Q* 5), exposed missing VGroup attributes (tax_wedge.qline, burden_split.pstar) that crashed Cue01/Cue02, and moved Cue12's question to the top band to clear the x-axis. Per-cue checklist (formula legibility+correctness incl P_b-P_s=tax and Q_tax<Q*, highlighted element matches narration, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing) all pass. Chunk timing verified in render log; final within Δ<0.5s of the 291.7s audio.
